tagline: Three truths, equal in weight, none of them kind.
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass with ice.
Stir for 25 rotations until well-chilled.
Strain into a rocks glass over one large cube.
Express an orange peel over the surface and drop it in.
omen: Bitterness is not the enemy. It is the thing that keeps sweetness honest. You will understand this by the second sip, and forgive someone by the third.

tagline: Announced by smoke before you see his face.
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a shaker with ice.
Shake hard for 12 seconds.
Strain into a rocks glass over fresh ice.
Garnish with a pineapple wedge.
omen: Tropical darkness. The Campari stains the pineapple like a letter written in haste, and you will taste regret and pleasure simultaneously. This is called Tuesday, in some latitudes.

tagline: A Negroni that went to law school.
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass with ice.
Stir for 30 rotations.
Strain into a coupe.
Express an orange peel over the drink; discard or drop in.
omen: Heavier than its cousin, and less willing to explain itself. The bourbon adds a closing argument the gin never had. You will settle an old debt tonight, if only with yourself.

tagline: Black clouds over a copper sea.
instructions: Fill a highball glass with ice.
Add ginger beer and lime juice.
Float the dark rum over the back of a barspoon.
Serve without stirring — let the storm settle on its own.
omen: Two layers that refuse to merge until you drink them. Spice from below, molasses from above. Something in your life is arranged the same way, and this drink will not tell you which thing, but you will know by the bottom of the glass.

tagline: Orange envelopes, unbroken seals, a sunlit afternoon.
instructions: Fill a large wine glass with ice.
Add the prosecco first, then the Aperol.
Top with club soda and stir once, gently.
Garnish with an orange slice.
omen: Good news is coming, if not for you then for someone whose happiness you can still borrow without shame. The bubbles rise in threes. Count them if you wish; it will not change what arrives.

tagline: Grapefruit, salt, and the memory of altitude.
instructions: Rim a highball glass with salt; fill with ice.
Add tequila, grapefruit juice, lime juice, and syrup.
Top with club soda.
Stir gently and garnish.
omen: Pink and salt and the sharp edge of the afternoon. You will make a small decision today that will look larger from a year away. The grapefruit knows this; that is why it is smiling.

tagline: Gin with its Sunday dress on.
instructions: Combine gin, lemon, and syrup in a shaker with ice.
Shake until thoroughly chilled.
Strain into a flute.
Top with cold Champagne.
omen: Lightness as a form of defiance. The acid sharpens the tongue and you will say the thing you have been practicing. It will land better than you hoped, and worse than they feared.

tagline: Mint and cane and the sound of ceiling fans.
instructions: Gently muddle the mint leaves with lime juice and syrup in a highball glass.
Add the rum and fill the glass with crushed ice.
Top with club soda.
Stir upward from the bottom to distribute the mint. Add more crushed ice to crown.
omen: The mint arrives before you are ready for it, as all green things do. Tonight you will laugh at something that is not a joke, and the person who said it will be grateful. Drink in the sun if you can find any.

tagline: Signed in a hand you almost recognize.
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass with ice.
Stir for 30 rotations until silky-cold.
Strain into a chilled coupe.
Garnish with a brandied cherry.
omen: The terms are favourable. The cost is deferred. You taste the rye, then the vermouth, then the quiet understanding that some agreements are signed in disappearing ink.

tagline: Five voices singing in a minor key.
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a mixing glass with ice.
Stir for 35 rotations until properly chilled.
Strain into a rocks glass over one large cube.
Express a lemon peel and discard.
omen: Five ingredients, none dominant, all necessary. This is how certain friendships work, and you have at least one that operates on exactly this principle. Call them tomorrow, not tonight. Tonight is for the drink.

tagline: The oldest question, asked properly.
instructions: Add syrup and bitters to a rocks glass.
Add one large ice cube.
Pour in the bourbon.
Stir gently in the glass for 15 rotations. Express an orange peel over the top.
omen: An old argument, gently resolved. Sip by sip the edges soften, and by the second glass you will have forgotten what was said, though not by whom. The ice melts at exactly the right pace if you let it.

tagline: Three ingredients with nothing to hide.
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a shaker with ice.
Shake hard for 10 seconds.
Double-strain into a chilled coupe.
Serve immediately — this drink waits for no one.
omen: Transparency. The lime cuts the sugar and the rum carries both without complaint. You will see something clearly tonight that you have been squinting at for weeks, and the seeing will be enough.

tagline: Honey-sealed, gin-delivered, unsigned.
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a shaker with ice.
Shake vigorously until the tin frosts.
Double-strain into a chilled coupe.
Express a lemon peel over the surface.
omen: The honey gentles the gin into something almost conversational, and conversations that begin this way tend to continue past the point of prudence. You have been warned, though not discouraged.

tagline: Blackberry ink on a map to nowhere pleasant.
instructions: Combine gin, lemon, and syrup in a shaker with ice.
Shake and strain into a rocks glass packed with crushed ice.
Drizzle the Creme de Mure over the top.
Let it bleed through the ice — do not stir.
omen: The purple bleeds downward like a secret told in the wrong order. Tart, then sweet, then the juniper reminds you where you started. You will return to a place you thought you were done with, and find it changed.

tagline: A letter from the sun, written in salt.
instructions: Rim half a rocks glass with flaky salt.
Combine tequila, lime, and Cointreau in a shaker with ice.
Shake hard until frost forms.
Strain into the prepared glass over fresh ice.
omen: Salt before citrus, citrus before agave, and the order matters more than anyone will admit. You will receive a message tonight, or you will send one. The salt is for courage; the lime is for clarity.

tagline: Cognac dressed for someone else's party.
instructions: Rim a coupe with fine sugar if desired.
Combine all ingredients in a shaker with ice.
Shake until thoroughly chilled.
Double-strain into the coupe.
omen: Something from another century, still perfectly dressed. The brandy wears the citrus like a borrowed coat, and you will feel, for one glass, like someone who has traveled more than they have. This is not deception; it is aspiration.

tagline: A patron saint for those who linger past good sense.
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a shaker with ice.
Shake vigorously until very cold.
Double-strain into a chilled coupe.
Serve with no garnish — the drink speaks for itself.
omen: Equal parts, all of them opinionated. The Chartreuse will not compromise and neither should you. This drink was built for the moment right before you say the thing you have been carrying all evening.

tagline: The dead do not stay down, and neither will you.
instructions: Rinse a coupe with the absinthe; discard the excess.
Combine remaining ingredients in a shaker with ice.
Shake until very cold.
Strain into the prepared coupe.
omen: What was behind you is now beside you. The absinthe is a rumor at the edge of every sip, and it will remind you of a morning you survived that you were not supposed to. You were supposed to. This drink knows it.

tagline: Lime, sugar, and the resolve of a machete.
instructions: Muddle the lime quarters with sugar in a rocks glass.
Add the cachaca.
Fill with crushed ice.
Stir vigorously to combine.
omen: Crude and undeniable. The sugar submits to the lime and the lime submits to the cachaca and none of them will apologize to you in the morning. Go ahead. You did not come here to be careful.

tagline: For the man who wanted his truth without sugar.
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a shaker with ice.
Shake hard for 10 seconds.
Double-strain into a chilled coupe.
Serve without garnish.
omen: Dry, tart, and honest in a way that hurts your teeth. The grapefruit is the bitterness you chose; the maraschino is the sweetness you pretend is not there. Write nothing tonight. Just drink and be still.

tagline: The cure that tastes like the disease, only kinder.
instructions: Combine the blended scotch, lemon, and honey-ginger syrup in a shaker with ice.
Shake until very cold.
Strain into a rocks glass over fresh ice.
Float the Laphroaig over the back of a barspoon.
omen: The ginger is the question; the smoke is the answer. You will sleep well tonight and the ache you brought with you will be halved by morning. The other half stays, but it was always yours to carry.

tagline: Honey and bourbon, holding a small light.
instructions: Combine all ingredients in a shaker with ice.
Shake until frost forms on the tin.
Strain into a rocks glass over one large cube.
No garnish needed.
omen: Amber in every direction. The honey rounds the bourbon into something almost maternal, and the lemon keeps it from becoming sentimental. You need this more than you think, and less than you fear.

tagline: A stay of execution, served with froth.
instructions: Combine bourbon, lemon, and syrup in a shaker with ice.
Shake hard until cold.
Strain into a rocks glass over fresh ice.
Dash bitters over the top.
omen: Sour, then sweet, then the oak underneath both like a floor you trust. You have done harder things than whatever brought you here tonight. The glass remembers even if you do not.

tagline: A walk taken slowly, with no destination.
instructions: Fill a highball glass with ice.
Pour in the Campari and vermouth.
Top with club soda.
Stir once and garnish.
omen: Low-proof and unhurried. The bitterness is present but not insistent, like a neighbor who waves but does not cross the street. You will go to bed at a reasonable hour, and this will be its own kind of victory.
